slides.title: The 60-second habit your *heart* notices
slides.sub: Daily flossing does more than protect your smile. The gums are a gateway to the rest of your body.

slides.title: Gums are a gateway
slides.body: Inflamed gums let oral bacteria like *P. gingivalis* slip into the bloodstream, where they can quietly stress the whole body.

slides.title: Bacteria reach the arteries
slides.body: Once in the blood, these bacteria *can inflame arteries*. Gum disease is linked to higher cardiovascular risk over time.

slides.title: It may reach the brain
slides.body: The same bacteria have been found in the brain. Healthier gums *are linked to* lower cognitive decline in some studies.

slides.title: Floss clears what brushing misses
slides.body: Brushing skips the spaces between teeth. *Flossing once a day* removes the plaque that feeds the bacteria at the gumline.

slides.title: Anchor it to a routine
slides.body: Attach flossing to something you already do, like brushing at night. A *tiny consistent habit* beats an occasional deep clean.

slides.title: Small habits, *compounded daily*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns the little things, like sleep, food, movement and steps, into one daily Health Score so good habits actually stick.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A minute of flossing is one of the most underrated things you can do for your heart and brain. 🦷🌿

Healthy gums keep bacteria like P. gingivalis out of your bloodstream, which is linked to lower cardiovascular and cognitive risk. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns small daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Flossing Daily Protects Your Heart and Brain
